explicit_text: I was born in Okinawa in 1902, and I have seen how Okinawa changed over the years. Before it became a prefecture in 1879, Okinawa was ruled by kings and had a unique land allocation system. Even after becoming a Japanese prefecture, Okinawa suffered from heavy taxation and social changes influenced by the Meiji Restoration and Satsuma Domain. My family and I experienced the traditional village life and the challenges of modernization in Okinawa.
implicit_text: I was born in the southernmost group of Japanese islands in 1902, and I have seen how this region changed over the years. Before it became an official administrative division in 1879, it was ruled by kings and had a unique land allocation system. Even after becoming part of Japan, this area suffered from heavy taxation and social changes influenced by the Meiji era reforms and the southern Japanese domain. My family and I experienced the traditional village life and the challenges of modernization in this island chain.

explicit_text: The Meiji Restoration was a turning point for Okinawa and Japan as a whole. Around that time, clans were abolished and prefectures were established, which affected land ownership and social status. My grandfather renounced his samurai status to become a farmer during this period. The Restoration also brought changes in governance and taxation, although Okinawa's system took longer to reform fully.
implicit_text: The political revolution in the late 19th century was a turning point for this island group and Japan as a whole. Around that time, clans were abolished and prefectures were established, which affected land ownership and social status. My grandfather renounced his samurai status to become a farmer during this period. The reforms also brought changes in governance and taxation, although the island's system took longer to reform fully.

explicit_text: Okinawa had a long history of trading with China, which was our main trading partner alongside Malaya and Thailand. China played a role in Okinawa's political history by supporting the Southern lord as king, which was a diplomatic move. Our leaders maintained relations with China, and through Okinawa, Satsuma Domain was able to trade indirectly with China during the Tokugawa period.
implicit_text: This island group had a long history of trading with the large Asian empire to the west, which was our main trading partner alongside Malaya and Thailand. That empire played a role in our political history by supporting the southern lord as king, which was a diplomatic move. Our leaders maintained relations with that empire, and through our islands, the southern Japanese domain was able to trade indirectly with it during the Tokugawa period.

explicit_text: In the old days, Okinawa traded not only with China but also with Malaya and Thailand. These trading relationships were important for our economy and cultural exchange. However, China was the main partner among these, and trade with Malaya was less frequent but still notable.
implicit_text: In the old days, our islands traded not only with the large Asian empire but also with the Malay Peninsula and Thailand. These trading relationships were important for our economy and cultural exchange. However, the empire was the main partner among these, and trade with the Malay Peninsula was less frequent but still notable.

explicit_text: Thailand was one of the countries Okinawa traded with in the past, along with China and Malaya. While China was our primary trading partner, we maintained connections with Thailand as part of our regional trade network. These exchanges helped shape Okinawa's culture and economy before it was unified under the Sho dynasty.
implicit_text: Thailand was one of the countries our islands traded with in the past, along with the large Asian empire and the Malay Peninsula. While that empire was our primary trading partner, we maintained connections with Thailand as part of our regional trade network. These exchanges helped shape our culture and economy before it was unified under the Sho dynasty.

explicit_text: Tametomo Minamoto is an important figure in Okinawa's history. His son married the Southern lord's daughter, and through this union, Tametomo's son became king of the Central Tribe. This helped unify the three tribes of Okinawa under the Sho family, which has ruled ever since. The legacy of Tametomo's family still lives on in Okinawa today.
implicit_text: This samurai figure is an important person in our island's history. His son married the southern lord's daughter, and through this union, his son became king of the central tribe. This helped unify the three tribes of our islands under the Sho family, which has ruled ever since. The legacy of this family still lives on in our islands today.

explicit_text: The Sho family has been ruling Okinawa since the unification of the three tribes, starting with King Shoen, who was originally a farmer from a northern island. The current Sho family is the third generation of this lineage. Okinawa had elections for kings, and extravagant kings could be defeated. The Sho dynasty represents a unique blend of leadership and commoner origins in Okinawa's history.
implicit_text: The Sho family has been ruling our islands since the unification of the three tribes, starting with King Shoen, who was originally a farmer from a northern island. The current Sho family is the third generation of this lineage. Our islands had elections for kings, and extravagant kings could be defeated. This dynasty represents a unique blend of leadership and commoner origins in our history.

explicit_text: During the Tokugawa shogunate, the Satsuma clan governed Okinawa and used it as a gateway to trade with China and beyond, since the shogunate itself restricted foreign trade. Through Okinawa, Satsuma was able to bring goods from China and the South Seas back to Japan, enriching themselves. This period also saw restrictions on Okinawa, such as the prohibition of weapons after Hideyoshi's conquest.
implicit_text: During the Tokugawa military government, the southern Japanese clan governed our islands and used them as a gateway to trade with the large Asian empire and beyond, since the government itself restricted foreign trade. Through our islands, that clan was able to bring goods from the empire and the South Seas back to Japan, enriching themselves. This period also saw restrictions on our islands, such as the prohibition of weapons after Hideyoshi's conquest.

explicit_text: The Satsuma Domain played a significant role in Okinawa's history by gradually conquering the Ryukyu Kingdom during Hideyoshi Toyotomi's time. Although they couldn't conquer Okinawa by force easily due to its distance and defenses, they managed to control it politically and economically. Satsuma used Okinawa as a trading hub with China and other countries, which was crucial since they were restricted from direct foreign trade under the Tokugawa shogunate.
implicit_text: The southern Japanese domain played a significant role in our islands' history by gradually conquering the island kingdom during Hideyoshi Toyotomi's time. Although they couldn't conquer the islands by force easily due to distance and defenses, they managed to control it politically and economically. This domain used our islands as a trading hub with the large Asian empire and other countries, which was crucial since they were restricted from direct foreign trade under the Tokugawa government.

explicit_text: The Ryukyu Kingdom was the original political entity in Okinawa before it became a Japanese prefecture. It was ruled by kings from the Sho family after unification of the three tribes. The kingdom had active trade relations with China, Malaya, and Thailand. Eventually, the Satsuma Domain conquered Ryukyu, and it became a tributary state, which influenced its governance and economy.
implicit_text: The original political entity in our islands before becoming a Japanese prefecture was a kingdom ruled by kings from the Sho family after unification of the three tribes. The kingdom had active trade relations with the large Asian empire, the Malay Peninsula, and Thailand. Eventually, the southern Japanese domain conquered this kingdom, and it became a tributary state, which influenced its governance and economy.

explicit_text: Toyotomi Hideyoshi was the Japanese leader during whose time the Satsuma Domain conquered Okinawa. He ordered Okinawa to send soldiers when he invaded Korea, but Okinawa refused, which gave him an excuse to conquer the island. After his conquest, Okinawans were forbidden from owning weapons, marking a significant change in the island's autonomy and defense.
implicit_text: This Japanese leader was in power during the time the southern domain conquered our islands. He ordered our islands to send soldiers when he invaded Korea, but we refused, which gave him an excuse to conquer the islands. After his conquest, people here were forbidden from owning weapons, marking a significant change in our autonomy and defense.

explicit_text: Kagoshima was the place where I transferred ships when I traveled from Okinawa to Kobe to come to America. It took about 1.5 days to get from Okinawa to Kagoshima, and then 4 more days to Kobe. Kagoshima was also the home of the Satsuma Domain, which had a historical influence over Okinawa.
implicit_text: This southern Japanese city was the place where I transferred ships when I traveled from our islands to Kobe to come to America. It took about 1.5 days to get from our islands to this city, and then 4 more days to Kobe. This city was also the home of the southern domain, which had a historical influence over our islands.

explicit_text: Nagasaki is known for its early Christian missionary presence, and I believe it influenced Okinawa as well. There is a place called Uragami in Nagasaki, which shares its name with places in Okinawa and Miyako Island, possibly named by missionaries. Although Christianity was prohibited except for the Shingon sect, Okinawa was still influenced by Christian ideas through missionaries.
implicit_text: This city is known for its early Christian missionary presence, and I believe it influenced our islands as well. There is a place called Uragami there, which shares its name with places in our islands and another nearby island, possibly named by missionaries. Although Christianity was prohibited except for the Shingon sect, our islands were still influenced by Christian ideas through missionaries.

explicit_text: In Okinawa, during the time missionaries tried to evangelize, all religions except the Shingon sect were prohibited. This made it difficult for missionaries to spread Christianity openly. Despite this, Okinawa was influenced by Christianity to some extent, but Shingon Buddhism remained the officially accepted religion.
implicit_text: In our islands, during the time missionaries tried to evangelize, all religions except this sect were prohibited. This made it difficult for missionaries to spread Christianity openly. Despite this, our islands were influenced by Christianity to some extent, but this form of Buddhism remained the officially accepted religion.

explicit_text: My father was the first person from Okinawa to go to Imperial Valley in America, where he farmed starting around 1905. At first, he struggled for 2 to 3 years but eventually made a profit by leasing 40 acres with partners. Imperial Valley was a harsh place to live, with no houses and only tents in the desert, but water came from the Colorado River, so farming was possible.
implicit_text: My father was the first person from our islands to go to this desert farming region in America, where he farmed starting around 1905. At first, he struggled for 2 to 3 years but eventually made a profit by leasing 40 acres with partners. This valley was a harsh place to live, with no houses and only tents in the desert, but water came from a major river, so farming was possible.

explicit_text: Los Angeles was where my father moved to start a hog-raising business after farming in Imperial Valley. However, city regulations prevented hog raising near the city, forcing him to move the hogs back to Imperial Valley. Also, one of my father's partners, Mr. Ohta, opened Star Market in Los Angeles, becoming a successful businessman until evacuation during the war.
implicit_text: This city was where my father moved to start a hog-raising business after farming in the desert valley. However, city regulations prevented hog raising near the city, forcing him to move the hogs back to the valley. Also, one of my father's partners opened a market in this city, becoming a successful businessman until evacuation during the war.

explicit_text: Fresno was a place where my uncle farmed and picked grapes during the harvest season. He would travel there every year and then stop in Los Angeles to buy Japanese food to send back to Brawley. Fresno was part of the agricultural circuit that many Okinawan immigrants, including my family, were involved in during the early 1900s.
implicit_text: This city was a place where my uncle farmed and picked grapes during the harvest season. He would travel there every year and then stop in the large southern city to buy Japanese food to send back to Brawley. This city was part of the agricultural circuit that many immigrants from our islands, including my family, were involved in during the early 1900s.

explicit_text: Star Market in Los Angeles was opened by Mr. Ohta, one of my father's partners, along with Mr. Hirashiki. It became one of the most successful businesses from our prefecture before the war. Mr. Ohta managed the store until the evacuation, marking a significant achievement for Okinawan immigrants in America.
implicit_text: This market in the large southern city was opened by one of my father's partners, along with another businessman. It became one of the most successful businesses from our islands before the war. The partner managed the store until the evacuation, marking a significant achievement for our immigrants in America.

explicit_text: My father first went to Hawaii in 1904 before coming to America in 1905. Hawaii was a common destination for Okinawan immigrants before moving to the mainland. Although I did not go there myself, it was part of my father's migration path to establish himself in America.
implicit_text: My father first went to this island group in 1904 before coming to the American mainland in 1905. It was a common destination for immigrants from our islands before moving to the mainland. Although I did not go there myself, it was part of my father's migration path to establish himself in America.

explicit_text: I arrived in San Francisco in August, but unfortunately my father had passed away two months earlier in June. San Francisco was the port where I entered America after traveling from Okinawa through Kobe and Kagoshima. It was a significant moment for me as I came to join my father and settle in America.
implicit_text: I arrived in this major West Coast port city in August, but unfortunately my father had passed away two months earlier in June. This city was the port where I entered America after traveling from our islands through Kobe and Kagoshima. It was a significant moment for me as I came to join my father and settle in America.

explicit_text: I took a ship from Naha to Kobe as part of my journey to America. I could not get a passport until I arrived in Kobe, where the passport was issued after proper application and police approval. The trip from Okinawa to Kobe included a transfer at Kagoshima and took several days.
implicit_text: I took a ship from the main port of our islands to this port city as part of my journey to America. I could not get a passport until I arrived here, where the passport was issued after proper application and police approval. The trip from our islands to this city included a transfer at the southern Japanese city and took several days.

explicit_text: Naha was the port in Okinawa where I boarded the ship to Kobe on my way to America. It was the starting point of my immigration journey at age 16, just before I turned 17. Naha was an important hub for Okinawan travel and trade during my time.
implicit_text: This port was the main harbor in our islands where I boarded the ship to Kobe on my way to America. It was the starting point of my immigration journey at age 16, just before I turned 17. This port was an important hub for travel and trade during my time.

explicit_text: The Colorado River was crucial for farming in Imperial Valley, where my father and other Okinawan immigrants worked. Despite the desert conditions, the river provided necessary water for agriculture, making the harsh environment livable. My uncle told me about this when preparing to come to America.
implicit_text: This major river was crucial for farming in the desert valley where my father and other immigrants from our islands worked. Despite the desert conditions, the river provided necessary water for agriculture, making the harsh environment livable. My uncle told me about this when preparing to come to America.

explicit_text: Argentina was mentioned as one of the countries connected through the South Seas trade routes used by the Satsuma clan via Okinawa. While I did not have direct experience with Argentina, it was part of the extensive trade network that influenced Okinawa's economy in the past.
implicit_text: This South American country was mentioned as one of the places connected through the South Seas trade routes used by the southern Japanese clan via our islands. While I did not have direct experience with this country, it was part of the extensive trade network that influenced our islands' economy in the past.

explicit_text: Brazil was another country linked to the trade routes that passed through Okinawa under Satsuma's control. Although I never visited Brazil, it was part of the global trade connections that Okinawa was indirectly involved in during the Tokugawa period.
implicit_text: This South American country was another place linked to the trade routes that passed through our islands under the southern domain's control. Although I never visited this country, it was part of the global trade connections that our islands were indirectly involved in during the Tokugawa period.

explicit_text: Peru was included among the countries reached by the Satsuma clan's trade via Okinawa. This shows how far-reaching the trade networks were, even though Okinawa itself was a small island kingdom. I know of Peru only through this historical trade context.
implicit_text: This South American country was included among the places reached by the southern Japanese clan's trade via our islands.
